title: myrsine lanaiensis
intro: Myrsine lanaiensis, also known as Ma'o hau hele, is a plant species native to Hawaii, belonging to the family Myrsinaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in Hawaiian medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which can be used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Myrsine lanaiensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In traditional Hawaiian herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, salves, and poultices, which are applied topically to promote healing and alleviate symptoms.
